questions.question: What does PEG stand for?
options: Personal eating and gastric device
Post-endoscopy gastric drainage
Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy
Peripheral enteral gastrostomy

questions.question: What does enteral feeding mean?
options: Giving nutrition directly into the stomach or gut rather than by mouth
Giving fluids into a vein through a drip
Encouraging a person to eat only soft foods
Feeding a person very slowly by spoon

questions.question: Where does a standard PEG tube deliver feed?
options: Into the small bowel beyond the stomach
Into the stomach
Into the bladder
Into a vein in the arm

questions.question: How does a PEG-J tube differ from a standard PEG?
options: It is only ever used for short-term feeding
It is passed through the nose instead of the abdomen
It has an extra portion that passes on from the stomach into the small bowel
It drains fluid out of the stomach rather than feeding in

questions.question: How is a nasogastric (NG) tube placed, and what is it usually used for?
options: Fitted over the mouth to help with swallowing
Placed through the abdomen for long-term feeding
Passed through the nose into the stomach, usually for shorter-term feeding
Inserted into the bowel for people who cannot tolerate stomach feed

questions.question: Why should a person be kept sitting upright during and after a PEG feed?
options: It uses gravity to help the feed stay down and reduces the risk of aspiration
It makes the feed flow faster so it finishes sooner
It is only necessary for people who can still eat by mouth
It removes the need to flush the tube with water

questions.question: What is aspiration?
options: Food or drink going into the airway and lungs instead of the stomach
The internal disc becoming stuck against the stomach wall
Extra red tissue growing at the stoma site
A blockage forming inside the feeding tube

questions.question: What does the term dysphagia mean?
options: Loss of appetite in older people
An infection at the stoma site
A dry, sore mouth caused by being nil by mouth
Difficulty swallowing

questions.question: A person with a PEG is nil by mouth. What does good practice include?
options: Skipping oral care because they are not eating
Providing regular oral care and giving nothing by mouth
Offering small sips of water to keep them hydrated
Giving tastes of food whenever they seem hungry

questions.question: You try to give a water flush and it will not go through the PEG, with unusual resistance. What is the safest action?
options: Give the feed anyway and hope it clears the blockage
Poke a thin object down the tube to clear it
Push hard on the syringe to force the blockage through
Do not force it, follow the gentle steps in your training, and escalate if it will not clear

questions.question: A person's PEG tube looks different from how the care plan describes it, and you are not sure it is safe to use. What should you do?
options: Start the feed anyway, as the person is due their nutrition
Stop, do not start the feed, and escalate to the registered nurse or dietitian
Try to push the tube back into position yourself
Wait until the end of your shift and mention it to the next carer

questions.question: Which of these is a sign of a possible PEG site infection that you should report?
options: Clean, dry, healthy-looking skin around the tube
The person asking what time their next feed is due
The external plate sitting in its usual, correct position
Redness, swelling, warmth or pain around the stoma, or discharge that may smell

questions.question: What is a buried bumper?
options: Feed leaking out around the outside of the tube
The external fixation plate pressing too tightly on the skin
The internal disc becoming embedded in the stomach wall
A blockage of thick feed inside the tube

questions.question: A PEG tube has been pulled out completely. Why is this treated as an emergency?
options: The person will feel hungry within a few minutes
The tube cannot be cleaned once it is outside the body
The feed pump will alarm continuously until it is switched off
The stoma can begin to close within hours

questions.question: What is the purpose of the water flushes given before and after feeds and medicines?
options: To warm the tube up before the feed starts
To keep the tube clear and help meet the person's fluid needs
To speed up how quickly the feed runs through
To replace the need for the person to have oral care

questions.question: What should you always do before you prepare or handle a feed?
options: Wash your hands thoroughly and put on a clean apron and gloves as directed
Warm the feed in hot water until it is very warm
Loosen the external fixation plate to relieve pressure
Lay the person flat so the feed runs in more easily

questions.question: What holds a PEG tube in place inside the stomach?
options: The external fixation plate alone
A small internal disc or balloon, sometimes called the bumper
Stitches through the skin that are removed after each feed
Suction created by the feed pump

questions.question: What is the stoma in relation to a PEG?
options: The bag that holds the prescribed feed
The clamp that stops feed flowing between feeds
The small tunnel through the abdominal wall where the tube passes
The camera used to place the tube

questions.question: Who makes the decision to feed someone enterally through a PEG?
options: The person's family members
Whoever is on shift when the person stops eating
The care worker on the person's next visit
Health professionals, often after a speech and language therapist assesses the swallow

questions.question: Under what conditions may a care worker carry out PEG feeding, flushing or giving medication through a PEG?
options: Whenever a colleague shows them how on the day
As soon as they have completed this awareness course
Only after task-specific training, assessed competence and delegation by a registered nurse or dietitian
Any time the person's family asks them to help

questions.question: Why are some PEG tubes gently rotated, and advanced and retracted, as part of routine care?
options: To stretch the stoma so the tube can be removed easily
To make the feed flow faster through the tube
To help stop the internal disc becoming stuck against the stomach wall (buried bumper)
To warm the tube before the feed begins

questions.question: What is granulation at a stoma site?
options: A blockage that has worked its way to the surface
The correct healthy appearance of a settled stoma
Extra red, moist, sometimes bleeding tissue that can grow at the stoma
A dry crust that always means infection is present

questions.question: According to the course, what can cause nausea, vomiting, bloating or diarrhoea in a person receiving a feed?
options: Keeping the person sitting upright during the feed
Providing regular oral care while nil by mouth
Flushing the tube before and after the feed
The feed running too fast, feed that is too cold, an intolerance, or another illness

questions.question: Which of these is a sign of dehydration to watch for and report?
options: The external plate resting gently in its usual position
A dry mouth, dark or reduced urine, drowsiness, confusion or sunken eyes
The feed running smoothly at its prescribed rate
Clean, dry skin around the stoma

questions.question: What is the correct approach to giving medicines through a PEG?
options: Medicines should be added to the feed bag to save time
Any tablet can be crushed and put down the tube by any carer
Medicines can be given down the tube without flushing before or after
Only a trained, delegated person may give medicines prescribed for that route, flushing between them

questions.question: Aspiration means food or drink going into the airway and lungs instead of the stomach.
options: True
False

questions.question: PEG feeding is a delegated clinical task requiring task-specific training, assessed competence and delegation by a registered nurse or dietitian.
options: True
False

questions.question: It is acceptable to offer small sips of water by mouth to a person who is nil by mouth, as long as the amount is small.
options: True
False

questions.question: If a PEG tube has been dislodged, a trained care worker should gently reinsert it themselves before restarting the feed.
options: True
False

questions.question: The skin around a PEG stoma should be cleaned and then dried gently and thoroughly to help prevent soreness and infection.
options: True
False

questions.question: If a tube seems blocked, you should force fluid down it with a syringe to push the blockage through.
options: True
False

questions.question: Water flushes should be given before and after feeds and medicines to help keep the tube clear.
options: True
False

questions.question: Oral care can be safely skipped when a person is nil by mouth because they are no longer eating.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of these are signs of a possible PEG site infection that you should record and report? (Select all that apply)
options: Swelling and warmth at the site
Clean, dry, healthy-looking skin
Redness around the stoma
Discharge that may smell
The external plate in its usual correct position

questions.question: Which of these are complications the course tells you to watch for in a person with a PEG? (Select all that apply)
options: Leakage of feed around the tube
The person asking about their next feed time
Granulation tissue at the stoma
Aspiration during feeding

questions.question: What must be in place before a care worker may carry out PEG feeding, flushing or medication through a PEG? (Select all that apply)
options: Task-specific training
Simply having completed this awareness course
Assessed competence
Delegation by a registered nurse or dietitian, set out in the care plan

questions.question: Which of these are signs of dehydration you should watch for and report? (Select all that apply)
options: Sunken eyes
Drowsiness or confusion
A dry mouth
Dark or reduced urine
Clean, dry skin around the stoma

questions.question: Which of these help prevent a PEG tube becoming blocked? (Select all that apply)
options: Flushing before and after every medicine
Giving only what is prescribed down the tube
Leaving thick feed or medicine sitting in the tube
Flushing with prescribed water before and after every feed

questions.question: Which of these are part of clean, safe practice when handling a feed? (Select all that apply)
options: Using feeds and giving sets well beyond the times specified
Working on a clean surface
Washing your hands thoroughly before and after handling the feed or tube
Wearing a clean apron and gloves as directed

questions.question: Which of these situations require urgent help, following your service's emergency procedure and dialling 999 if life is at risk? (Select all that apply)
options: A tube that has fallen out
Signs of choking or serious breathing difficulty
Severe abdominal pain
The person asking what time their feed is due
Bleeding from the site

questions.question: Keep the person sitting ___ during and after a feed to reduce aspiration risk.
Difficulty swallowing is known as ___.
If a PEG tube falls out, the stoma can begin to close within ___, so this is an emergency.
Water ___ are given before and after feeds and medicines to keep the tube clear.
A serious problem where the internal disc becomes stuck against the stomach wall is called a ___ bumper.
